overview: The IDML Filter processes Adobe InDesign IDML documents (InDesign Markup Language), an XML-based format introduced in InDesign CS4. The filter extracts text by spread, gathering stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements in spread order. IDML documents are ZIP packages (UCF) containing multiple sub-documents, each treated as a separate sub-document by the filter. The [IDML specification](http://www.adobe.com/content/dam/Adobe/en/devnet/indesign/cs5_docs/idml/idml-specification.pdf) is available on the Adobe Web site.

maxAttributeSize.help: Controls the attribute buffer size in MB. Increase this if processing IDML documents with very large XML attributes that exceed the default 4 MB limit.
maxAttributeSize.values: Integer value in MB. Default is `4` (4 × 1024 × 1024 bytes).

specialCharacterPattern.help: Regex pattern matching characters that should be treated as **inline codes** rather than translatable text. The default pattern matches various Unicode special characters including zero-width spaces, zero-width non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, and BOM characters.

untagXmlStructures.help: Strips embedded XML structural information from the extracted content. Use this when the IDML document contains XML tagging that is not relevant for translation and would clutter the translatable segments.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: Combines consecutive inline codes into a single code. This reduces the number of inline tags translators need to manage, making segments cleaner and easier to translate.

extractNotes.help: Includes content from `<Note>` elements in the extraction. By default, notes are skipped during extraction.

extractMasterSpreads.help: Includes content from master spreads in the extraction. By default, only normal spreads are processed. Enable this if master spreads contain translatable text (e.g., headers, footers, or recurring labels).

extractHiddenLayers.help: Includes content from hidden layers in the extraction. By default, only visible layers are extracted.

extractHiddenPasteboardItems.help: Includes content from items on the pasteboard (outside the visible page area) that are hidden. These are typically elements placed outside the page bounds during layout editing.

skipDiscretionaryHyphens.help: Omits discretionary (soft) hyphens from the extracted text. Discretionary hyphens are hints for line-breaking that may not be appropriate in translated text with different word lengths.

extractBreaksInline.help: Treats break elements as inline codes within text units rather than creating separate segments at break boundaries.

extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline.help:
Controls how hyperlink text sources are represented in the extraction.

- **Enabled**: Hyperlink text sources are extracted as inline elements within the surrounding text.
- **Disabled** (default): Hyperlink text sources are represented as referencing groups of separate textual units.

extractCustomTextVariables.help: Includes custom text variables defined in the InDesign document in the extraction for translation.

extractIndexTopics.help: Includes index topic entries in the extraction for translation.

extractExternalHyperlinks.help: Includes external hyperlink URLs/text in the extraction for translation. Enable this when hyperlink destinations need to be localized (e.g., region-specific URLs).

extractMathZones.help: Includes math zone content in the extraction for translation. Enabled by default. Disable this if math expressions should be left untranslated.

excludedStyles.help: Specifies paragraph or character style names whose content should be **excluded** from extraction. Use this to skip non-translatable styled content such as code listings, product IDs, or other fixed-text styles.
excludedStyles.values: List of InDesign style names to exclude from extraction.

ignoreCharacterKerning.help: Prevents character kerning differences from creating separate inline codes. When characters differ only in kerning values, they are treated as the same run of text, reducing unnecessary inline code segmentation.

ignoreCharacterTracking.help: Prevents character tracking (letter-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Reduces inline code clutter when tracking varies across a text run.

ignoreCharacterLeading.help: Prevents character leading (line-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Reduces inline code clutter when leading varies within a paragraph.

ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.help: Prevents baseline shift differences from creating separate inline codes. Useful when characters have varying baseline offsets (e.g., for visual alignment) that should not affect segmentation.

processingNotes: The input encoding is **automatically detected**; any user-specified encoding is ignored. The filter always uses UTF-8.
Output line-breaks are always set to simple linefeed (LF).

An IDML document is a ZIP package (UCF — Universal Container Format) containing multiple embedded documents. Each embedded file is treated as a **sub-document** — e.g., in XLIFF output, a single IDML produces multiple `<file>` elements.
Text is extracted **by spread**: for each spread, stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements are gathered and extracted in the order they appear.
Stories embedded inside other stories and not declared at a spread level are extracted in a special group.
The filter is actually a wrapper around `IDMLContentFilter` — the inner filter handles raw IDML XML (Story XML) files directly, while the outer `IDMLFilter` handles the full IDML package.
limitations: IDML filter is marked as **ALPHA** in the Okapi help documentation.

examples.title: Extract with hidden layers and master spreads
examples.description: Configuration to extract all content including hidden layers and master spreads, useful for comprehensive translation of an InDesign document.
examples.config:
```yaml
extractMasterSpreads: true
extractHiddenLayers: true
extractNotes: true
```

examples.title: Clean extraction with reduced inline codes
examples.description: Configuration that minimizes inline code clutter by merging adjacent codes and ignoring formatting-only differences.
examples.config:
```yaml
mergeAdjacentCodes: true
ignoreCharacterKerning: true
ignoreCharacterTracking: true
ignoreCharacterLeading: true
ignoreCharacterBaselineShift: true
```

maxAttributeSize.description: Size in MB for the attribute buffer (default 4 MB).

specialCharacterPattern.description: Pattern matching characters treated as inline codes.

mergeAdjacentCodes.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single code.

skipDiscretionaryHyphens.description: Omit discretionary (soft) hyphens from extracted text.

extractBreaksInline.description: Treat break elements as inline codes within text units.

extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline.title: Extract Hyperlink Sources Inline
extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline.description: Extract hyperlink text sources as inline elements.

extractCustomTextVariables.title: Extract Custom Text Variables
extractCustomTextVariables.description: Include custom text variables in the extraction.

extractIndexTopics.title: Extract Index Topics
extractIndexTopics.description: Include index topic entries in the extraction.

extractExternalHyperlinks.description: Extract external hyperlink URLs for translation.

extractMathZones.title: Extract Math Zones
extractMathZones.description: Include math zone content in the extraction.

excludedStyles.title: Excluded Styles
excludedStyles.description: Styles whose content is excluded from extraction.

ignoreCharacterKerning.title: Ignore Character Kerning
ignoreCharacterKerning.description: Prevent kerning differences from creating separate inline codes.

ignoreCharacterTracking.title: Ignore Character Tracking
ignoreCharacterTracking.description: Prevent tracking differences from creating separate inline codes.

ignoreCharacterLeading.description: Prevent leading differences from creating separate inline codes.

ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.description: Prevent baseline shift differences from creating separate inline codes.

fullDoc:
# IDML Filter

The IDML Filter processes Adobe InDesign IDML documents (InDesign Markup Language), an XML-based format introduced in InDesign CS4. The filter extracts text by spread, gathering stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements in spread order. IDML documents are ZIP packages (UCF) containing multiple sub-documents, each treated as a separate sub-document by the filter.

## Parameters

### General

#### Maximum Attribute Size
Controls the attribute buffer size in MB. Increase this if processing IDML documents with very large XML attributes that exceed the default 4 MB limit. Default: **4 MB**.

#### Special Character Pattern
Regex pattern matching characters that should be treated as **inline codes** rather than translatable text. The default pattern matches various Unicode special characters including zero-width spaces, zero-width non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, and BOM characters.

#### Untag XML Structures
Strips embedded XML structural information from the extracted content. Use this when the IDML document contains XML tagging that is not relevant for translation.

#### Merge Adjacent Codes
Combines consecutive inline codes into a single code, reducing tag clutter for translators. Default: **false**.

### Extraction

#### Extract Notes
Includes content from `<Note>` elements in the extraction. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Master Spreads
Includes content from master spreads in the extraction. Enable this if master spreads contain translatable text (e.g., headers, footers, recurring labels). Default: **false**.

#### Extract Hidden Layers
Includes content from hidden layers in the extraction. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Hidden Pasteboard Items
Includes content from items on the pasteboard (outside the visible page area) that are hidden. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Breaks Inline
Treats break elements as inline codes within text units rather than creating separate segments. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Hyperlink Text Sources Inline
Controls how hyperlink text sources are represented:
- **Enabled**: Hyperlink text sources are extracted as inline elements within the surrounding text.
- **Disabled** (default): Hyperlink text sources are represented as referencing groups of separate textual units.

#### Extract Custom Text Variables
Includes custom text variables defined in the InDesign document. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Index Topics
Includes index topic entries in the extraction. Default: **false**.

#### Extract External Hyperlinks
Includes external hyperlink URLs/text for translation. Enable when hyperlink destinations need to be localized. Default: **false**.

#### Extract Math Zones
Includes math zone content for translation. Default: **true**.

#### Excluded Styles
Specifies paragraph or character style names whose content should be excluded from extraction. Use this to skip non-translatable styled content such as code listings or product IDs.

### Formatting

#### Skip Discretionary Hyphens
Omits discretionary (soft) hyphens from extracted text. Default: **false**.

#### Ignore Character Kerning
Prevents kerning differences from creating separate inline codes. Default: **false**.

#### Ignore Character Tracking
Prevents tracking (letter-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Default: **false**.

#### Ignore Character Leading
Prevents leading (line-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Default: **false**.

#### Ignore Character Baseline Shift
Prevents baseline shift differences from creating separate inline codes. Default: **false**.

## Processing Notes

- Input encoding is **automatically detected**; user-specified encoding is ignored. Always uses UTF-8.
- Output line-breaks are always simple linefeed (LF).
- IDML documents are ZIP packages (UCF). Each embedded file is treated as a sub-document.
- Text is extracted by spread, with stories gathered from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements.
- Stories embedded inside other stories and not declared at spread level are extracted in a special group.
- The filter wraps `IDMLContentFilter` — the inner filter handles raw Story XML files directly.

## Limitations

- The IDML filter is marked as **ALPHA** in the Okapi help documentation.

## Examples

### Clean extraction with reduced inline codes

Minimize inline code clutter by merging adjacent codes and ignoring formatting-only differences:

```yaml
mergeAdjacentCodes: true
ignoreCharacterKerning: true
ignoreCharacterTracking: true
ignoreCharacterLeading: true
ignoreCharacterBaselineShift: true
```

### Full content extraction

Extract all content including hidden layers, master spreads, and notes:

```yaml
extractMasterSpreads: true
extractHiddenLayers: true
extractNotes: true
extractCustomTextVariables: true
extractIndexTopics: true
```
